summary: noetl#430 was merged and ai-meta now points repos/noetl at post-#430 main. GKE was still configured for the stale in-cluster MinIO endpoint, so the Helm object-store chooser was enabled with SeaweedFS as the default backend and NoETL server/worker were repointed to the canonical object-store Service. A worker-restart durability test passed on GKE, and the travel activities path completed with an app:column widget plus travel_agent_events audit rows. GUI screenshot capture was not completed because the available browser surface was the Cloudflare login page; backend/API evidence is recorded instead.

before.visible_minio_or_object_store_workloads: No MinIO workload or object-store Service was visible before the chooser deploy.

phase_results.name: deploy_object_store_chooser
phase_results.note: ConfigMap changes required explicit noetl-server/noetl-worker rollout restarts before the pods picked up the new endpoint.

phase_results.name: worker_restart_durability_test
phase_results.method: boto3 put/get inside noetl-worker, rollout restart deployment/noetl-worker, boto3 get again from the restarted worker deployment

phase_results.name: travel_agent_activities_smoke
phase_results.note: The agent executor first logged a local disk-cache miss for the child reference, then the post-#430 terminal-event fallback returned the bounded child data and the parent rendered successfully. This is the expected engine-fix behavior.

open_items.reason: The currently available browser surface is https://mestumre.dev/login. No authenticated GUI screenshot was captured in this backend rollout round.
Unchanged from prior parity work; ollama-bridge is not deployed on GKE and remains a separate infra round.

secrets_hygiene: No secret values are intentionally written in this result file. Raw execution status responses can expose resolved keychain data, so close-out evidence was summarized from sanitized event and audit queries only.
